name: CpuSample
desc: See VmService.getCpuSamples and CpuSamples.

The identityHashCode assigned to the allocated object. This hash code is
the same as the hash code provided in HeapSnapshot. Provided for CpuSample
instances returned from a getAllocationTraces().

name: stack
desc:
The call stack at the time this sample was collected. The stack is to be
interpreted as top to bottom. Each element in this array is a key into the
functions array in CpuSamples.

name: tid
desc: The thread ID representing the thread on which this sample was collected.

name: timestamp
desc: The time this sample was collected in microseconds.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: truncated
desc:
Provided and set to true if the sample's stack was truncated. This can
happen if the stack is deeper than the stackDepth in the CpuSamples
response.

name: userTag
desc:
The name of the User tag set when this sample was collected. Omitted if no
User tag was set when this sample was collected.

name: vmTag
desc:
The name of VM tag set when this sample was collected. Omitted if the VM
tag for the sample is not considered valid.

name: CpuSamples
desc: See VmService.getCpuSamples and CpuSample.

name: functions

which function was on the stack.

name: maxStackDepth
desc: The maximum possible stack depth for samples.

name: pid
desc: The process ID for the VM.

name: sampleCount
desc: The number of samples returned.

name: samplePeriod
desc: The sampling rate for the profiler in microseconds.

name: samples
desc: A list of samples collected in the range [timeOriginMicros, timeOriginMicros + timeExtentMicros]

name: timeExtentMicros
desc: The duration of time covered by the returned samples.

name: timeOriginMicros
desc:
The start of the period of time in which the returned samples were
collected.

name: clearHttpProfile
desc:
The clearHttpProfile RPC is used to clear previously recorded HTTP
requests from the HTTP profiler state. Requests still in-flight after
clearing the profiler state will be ignored by the profiler.

name: clearSocketProfile
desc: Removes all statistics associated with prior and current sockets.

name: getDartIOVersion
desc:
The getDartIOVersion RPC returns the available version of the dart:io
service protocol extensions.

name: getHttpProfile
desc:
The getHttpProfile RPC is used to retrieve HTTP profiling information
for requests made via dart:io's HttpClient.

name: getHttpProfileRequest
desc:
The getHttpProfileRequest RPC is used to retrieve an instance of
HttpProfileRequest, which includes request and response body data.

name: getOpenFileById
desc:
The getOpenFileById RPC is used to retrieve information about files
currently opened by dart:io from a given isolate.

name: getOpenFiles
desc:
The getOpenFiles RPC is used to retrieve the list of files currently
opened files by dart:io from a given isolate.

name: getSocketProfile
desc:
The getSocketProfile RPC is used to retrieve socket statistics collected
by the socket profiler. Only samples collected after the initial
socketProfilingEnabled call or the last call to clearSocketProfile
will be reported.

name: getSpawnedProcessById
desc:
The getSpawnedProcessById RPC is used to retrieve information about a process
spawned by dart:io from a given isolate.

name: getSpawnedProcesses
desc:
The getSpawnedProcesses RPC is used to retrieve the list of processed opened
by dart:io from a given isolate

name: httpEnableTimelineLogging
desc:
The httpEnableTimelineLogging RPC is used to set and inspect the value of
HttpClient.enableTimelineLogging, which determines if HTTP client requests
should be logged to the timeline. If enabled is provided, the state of
HttpClient.enableTimelineLogging will be updated to the value of enabled.

name: isHttpProfilingAvailable
desc: Whether HTTP profiling is available for the given isolateId.

name: isHttpTimelineLoggingAvailable
desc: Whether HTTP timeline logging is available for the given isolateId.

name: isSocketProfilingAvailable
desc: Whether socket profiling is available for the given isolateId.

name: socketProfilingEnabled
desc:
The socketProfilingEnabled RPC is used to enable/disable the socket profiler
and query its current state. If enabled is provided, the profiler state will
be updated to reflect the value of enabled.

name: Error

An Error represents a Dart language level error. This is distinct from an
RPCError.

name: message

desc: A string representation of this object.

name: valueAsString
desc: A reasonable string representation of this sentinel.

name: SentinelException
desc: Thrown when an RPC response is a Sentinel.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: SentinelKind
desc:
A SentinelKind is used to distinguish different kinds of Sentinel
objects.

name: kBeingInitialized
desc: Deprecated, no longer used.

name: kCollected
desc: Indicates that the object referred to has been collected by the GC.

name: kExpired
desc: Indicates that an object id has expired.

name: kFree
desc: Reserved for future use.

name: kNotInitialized
desc: Indicates that a variable or field has not been initialized.

name: kOptimizedOut
desc: Indicates that a variable has been eliminated by the optimizing compiler.

name: SocketProfile
desc: A SocketProfile provides information about statistics of sockets.

name: sockets
desc: List of socket statistics.

name: SocketProfilingState
desc:
A SocketProfilingState provides information about the current state of
socket profiling for a given isolate.

name: address
desc: The address of the socket.

name: endTime
desc: The time, in microseconds, that this socket was closed.

name: id
desc: The unique ID associated with this socket.

name: lastReadTime
desc: The time, in microseconds, that this socket was last read from.

name: lastWriteTime
desc: The time, in microseconds, that this socket was last written to.

name: port
desc: The port of the socket.

name: readBytes
desc: The number of bytes read from this socket.

name: socketType
desc: The type of socket. The value is either tcp or udp.

name: startTime
desc: The time, in microseconds, that this socket was created.

name: writeBytes
desc: The number of bytes written to this socket.

name: SourceLocation
desc:
The SourceLocation class is used to designate a position or range in some
script.

name: column
desc:
The column associated with this location. Only provided for non-synthetic
token positions.

name: endTokenPos
desc: The last token of the location if this is a range.

name: line
desc:
The line associated with this location. Only provided for non-synthetic
token positions.

name: script
desc: The script containing the source location.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: tokenPos
desc: The first token of the location.

name: SourceReport
desc:
The SourceReport class represents a set of reports tied to source
locations in an isolate.

name: ranges
desc:
A list of ranges in the program source.  These ranges correspond to ranges
of executable code in the user's program (functions, methods,
constructors, etc.)

name: scripts
desc: A list of scripts, referenced by index in the report's ranges.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: SourceReportCoverage
desc:
The SourceReportCoverage class represents coverage information for one
SourceReportRange.

name: hits
desc:
A list of token positions (or line numbers if reportLines was enabled) in
a SourceReportRange which have been executed.  The list is sorted.

name: misses
desc:
A list of token positions (or line numbers if reportLines was enabled) in
a SourceReportRange which have not been executed.  The list is sorted.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: kBranchCoverage
desc: Used to request branch coverage information.

name: kCoverage
desc: Used to request a code coverage information.

name: kPossibleBreakpoints
desc: Used to request a list of token positions of possible breakpoints.

name: SourceReportRange
desc:
The SourceReportRange class represents a range of executable code
(function, method, constructor, etc) in the running program. It is part of a
SourceReport.

name: branchCoverage
desc:
Branch coverage information for this range.  Provided only when the
BranchCoverage report has been requested and the range has been compiled.

name: compiled
desc: Has this range been compiled by the Dart VM?

name: coverage
desc:
Code coverage information for this range.  Provided only when the Coverage
report has been requested and the range has been compiled.

name: endPos
desc: The token position at which this range ends.  Inclusive.

name: error
desc:
The error while attempting to compile this range, if this report was
generated with forceCompile=true.

name: possibleBreakpoints
desc:
Possible breakpoint information for this range, represented as a sorted
list of token positions (or line numbers if reportLines was enabled).
Provided only when the when the PossibleBreakpoint report has been
requested and the range has been compiled.

name: scriptIndex
desc:
An index into the script table of the SourceReport, indicating which
script contains this range of code.

name: startPos
desc: The token position at which this range begins.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: SpawnedProcess
desc: A SpawnedProcess contains startup information of a spawned process.

name: arguments
desc: The list of arguments provided to the process at launch.

name: id
desc: The unique ID associated with this process.

name: name
desc: The name of the executable.

name: pid
desc: The process ID associated with the process.

name: startedAt
desc: The time the process was started in milliseconds since epoch.

name: workingDirectory
desc: The working directory of the process at launch.

name: processes
desc: A list of processes spawned through dart:io on a given isolate.

name: SpawnedProcessRef
desc:
A SpawnedProcessRef contains identifying information about a spawned
process.

name: id
desc: The unique ID associated with this process.

name: name
desc: The name of the executable.

name: Stack
desc:
The Stack class represents the various components of a Dart stack trace
for a given isolate.

name: asyncCausalFrames
desc:
A list of frames which contains both synchronous part and the asynchronous
continuation e.g. async functions awaiting completion of the currently
running async function. Asynchronous frames are separated from each
other and synchronous prefix via frames of kind
FrameKind.kAsyncSuspensionMarker.

name: awaiterFrames
desc:
Deprecated since version 4.7 of the protocol. Will be always absent in the
response.

name: frames
desc:
A list of frames that make up the synchronous stack, rooted at the message
loop (i.e., the frames since the last asynchronous gap or the isolate's
entrypoint).

name: messages
desc: A list of messages in the isolate's message queue.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: truncated
desc:
Specifies whether or not this stack is complete or has been artificially
truncated.

name: StepOption
desc:
A StepOption indicates which form of stepping is requested in a resume
RPC.

name: Success
desc:
The Success type is used to indicate that an operation completed
successfully.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: Timeline
desc: See VmService.getVMTimeline;

name: timeExtentMicros
desc: The duration of time covered by the timeline.

name: timeOriginMicros
desc: The start of the period of time in which traceEvents were collected.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: traceEvents
desc:
A list of timeline events. No order is guaranteed for these events; in
particular, these events may be unordered with respect to their
timestamps.

name: TimelineEvent
desc:
An TimelineEvent is an arbitrary map that contains a Trace Event Format
event.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: availableStreams
desc: The list of all available timeline streams.

name: recordedStreams
desc: The list of timeline streams that are currently enabled.

name: recorderName
desc:
The name of the recorder currently in use. Recorder types include, but are
not limited to: Callback, Endless, Fuchsia, Macos, Ring, Startup, and
Systrace. Set to "null" if no recorder is currently set.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: timestamp
desc: A timestamp in microseconds since epoch.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: TypeArguments
desc:
A TypeArguments object represents the type argument vector for some
instantiated generic type.

name: hashCode
desc: The hash code for this object.

name: name
desc: A name for this type argument list.

name: operator ==
desc: The equality operator.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: types
desc: A list of types.

name: TypeArgumentsRef
desc: TypeArgumentsRef is a reference to a TypeArguments object.

name: hashCode
desc: The hash code for this object.

name: name
desc: A name for this type argument list.

name: operator ==
desc: The equality operator.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: TypeParameters
desc:
A TypeParameters object represents the type argument vector for some
uninstantiated generic type.

name: bounds
desc: The bounds set on each type parameter.

name: defaults
desc: The default types for each type parameter.

name: hashCode
desc: The hash code for this object.

name: names
desc: The names of the type parameters.

name: operator ==
desc: The equality operator.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: TypeParametersRef
desc: TypeParametersRef is a reference to a TypeParameters object.

name: hashCode
desc: The hash code for this object.

name: operator ==
desc: The equality operator.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: UnresolvedSourceLocation
desc:
The UnresolvedSourceLocation class is used to refer to an unresolved
breakpoint location. As such, it is meant to approximate the final location
of the breakpoint but it is not exact.

name: column
desc:
An approximate column number for the source location. This may change when
the location is resolved.

name: line
desc:
An approximate line number for the source location. This may change when
the location is resolved.

name: script
desc: The script containing the source location if the script has been loaded.

name: scriptUri
desc:
The uri of the script containing the source location if the script has yet
to be loaded.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: tokenPos
desc:
An approximate token position for the source location. This may change
when the location is resolved.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: uris
desc: A list of URIs.

name: architectureBits
desc: Word length on target architecture (e.g. 32, 64).

name: hostCPU
desc: The CPU we are actually running on.

name: isolateGroups
desc: A list of isolate groups running in the VM.

name: isolates
desc: A list of isolates running in the VM.

name: name
desc: A name identifying this vm. Not guaranteed to be unique.

name: operatingSystem
desc: The operating system we are running on.

name: pid
desc: The process id for the VM.

name: startTime
desc: The time that the VM started in milliseconds since the epoch.

name: systemIsolateGroups
desc: A list of isolate groups which contain system isolates running in the VM.

name: systemIsolates
desc: A list of system isolates running in the VM.

name: targetCPU
desc: The CPU we are generating code for.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: version
desc: The Dart VM version string.

name: VMRef
desc: VMRef is a reference to a VM object.

name: name
desc: A name identifying this vm. Not guaranteed to be unique.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: Version
desc: See Versioning.

name: major
desc:
The major version number is incremented when the protocol is changed in a
potentially incompatible way.

name: minor
desc:
The minor version number is incremented when the protocol is changed in a
backwards compatible way.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: addBreakpoint
desc:
The addBreakpoint RPC is used to add a breakpoint at a specific line of
some script.

name: addBreakpointAtEntry
desc:
The addBreakpointAtEntry RPC is used to add a breakpoint at the
entrypoint of some function.

name: addBreakpointWithScriptUri
desc:
The addBreakpoint RPC is used to add a breakpoint at a specific line of
some script. This RPC is useful when a script has not yet been assigned an
id, for example, if a script is in a deferred library which has not yet
been loaded.

name: callMethod
desc:
Call an arbitrary service protocol method. This allows clients to call
methods not explicitly exposed by this library.

name: callServiceExtension
desc: Invoke a specific service protocol extension method.

name: clearCpuSamples
desc: Clears all CPU profiling samples.

name: clearVMTimeline
desc: Clears all VM timeline events.

name: createIdZone
desc:
The createIdZone RPC is used to create a new ID zone where temporary IDs
for instances in the specified isolate may be allocated. See IDs and
Names for more information about ID zones.

name: deleteIdZone
desc:
The deleteIdZone RPC frees the buffer that backs the specified ID zone,
and makes that zone unusable for the remainder of the program's execution.
For performance reasons, clients should aim to call invalidateIdZone and
reuse existing zones as much as possible instead of deleting zones and
then creating new ones.

name: evaluate
desc:
The evaluate RPC is used to evaluate an expression in the context of
some target.

name: evaluateInFrame
desc:
The evaluateInFrame RPC is used to evaluate an expression in the context
of a particular stack frame. frameIndex is the index of the desired
Frame, with an index of 0 indicating the top (most recent) frame.

name: getAllocationProfile
desc:
The getAllocationProfile RPC is used to retrieve allocation information
for a given isolate.

name: getAllocationTraces
desc:
The getAllocationTraces RPC allows for the retrieval of allocation
traces for objects of a specific set of types (see
VmService.setTraceClassAllocation). Only samples collected in the time
range [timeOriginMicros, timeOriginMicros + timeExtentMicros] will be
reported.

name: getClassList
desc:
The getClassList RPC is used to retrieve a ClassList containing all
classes for an isolate based on the isolate's isolateId.

name: getCpuSamples
desc:
The getCpuSamples RPC is used to retrieve samples collected by the CPU
profiler. See CpuSamples for a detailed description of the response.

name: getFlagList
desc:
The getFlagList RPC returns a list of all command line flags in the VM
along with their current values.

name: getInboundReferences
desc:
Returns a set of inbound references to the object specified by targetId.
Up to limit references will be returned.

name: getInstances
desc:
The getInstances RPC is used to retrieve a set of instances which are of
a specific class.

name: getInstancesAsList
desc:
The getInstancesAsList RPC is used to retrieve a set of instances which
are of a specific class. This RPC returns an InstanceRef corresponding
to a Dart List<dynamic> that contains the requested instances. This
List is not growable, but it is otherwise mutable. The response type is
what distinguishes this RPC from getInstances, which returns an
InstanceSet.

name: getIsolate
desc: The getIsolate RPC is used to lookup an Isolate object by its id.

name: getIsolateGroup
desc:
The getIsolateGroup RPC is used to lookup an IsolateGroup object by
its id.

name: getIsolateGroupMemoryUsage
desc:
The getIsolateGroupMemoryUsage RPC is used to lookup an isolate group's
memory usage statistics by its id.

name: getIsolatePauseEvent
desc:
The getIsolatePauseEvent RPC is used to lookup an isolate's pause event
by its id.

name: getMemoryUsage
desc:
The getMemoryUsage RPC is used to lookup an isolate's memory usage
statistics by its id.

name: getObject
desc:
The getObject RPC is used to lookup an object from some isolate by its
id.

name: getPerfettoCpuSamples
desc:
The getPerfettoCpuSamples RPC is used to retrieve samples collected by
the CPU profiler, serialized in Perfetto's proto format. See
PerfettoCpuSamples for a detailed description of the response.

name: getPerfettoVMTimeline
desc:
The getPerfettoVMTimeline RPC is used to retrieve an object which
contains a VM timeline trace represented in Perfetto's proto format. See
PerfettoTimeline for a detailed description of the response.

name: getPorts
desc:
The getPorts RPC is used to retrieve the list of ReceivePort instances
for a given isolate.

name: getProcessMemoryUsage
desc: Returns a description of major uses of memory known to the VM.

name: getQueuedMicrotasks
desc:
The getQueuedMicrotasks RPC returns a snapshot containing information
about the microtasks that were queued in the specified isolate when the
snapshot was taken.

name: getRetainingPath
desc:
The getRetainingPath RPC is used to lookup a path from an object
specified by targetId to a GC root (i.e., the object which is preventing
this object from being garbage collected).

name: getScripts
desc:
The getScripts RPC is used to retrieve a ScriptList containing all
scripts for an isolate based on the isolate's isolateId.

name: getSourceReport
desc:
The getSourceReport RPC is used to generate a set of reports tied to
source locations in an isolate.

name: getStack
desc:
The getStack RPC is used to retrieve the current execution stack and
message queue for an isolate. The isolate does not need to be paused.

name: getSupportedProtocols
desc:
The getSupportedProtocols RPC is used to determine which protocols are
supported by the current server.

name: getVM
desc: The getVM RPC returns global information about a Dart virtual machine.

name: getVMTimeline
desc:
The getVMTimeline RPC is used to retrieve an object which contains VM
timeline events. See Timeline for a detailed description of the
response.

name: getVMTimelineFlags
desc:
The getVMTimelineFlags RPC returns information about the current VM
timeline configuration.

name: getVMTimelineMicros
desc:
The getVMTimelineMicros RPC returns the current time stamp from the
clock used by the timeline, similar to Timeline.now in dart:developer
and Dart_TimelineGetMicros in the VM embedding API.

name: getVersion
desc:
The getVersion RPC is used to determine what version of the Service
Protocol is served by a VM.

name: invalidateIdZone
desc:
The invalidateIdZone RPC is used to invalidate all the IDs that have
been allocated in a certain ID zone. Invaliding the IDs makes them expire.
See IDs and Names for more information.

name: invoke
desc:
The invoke RPC is used to perform regular method invocation on some
receiver, as if by dart:mirror's ObjectMirror.invoke. Note this does not
provide a way to perform getter, setter or constructor invocation.

name: kill
desc:
The kill RPC is used to kill an isolate as if by dart:isolate's
Isolate.kill(IMMEDIATE).

name: lookupPackageUris
desc:
The lookupPackageUris RPC is used to convert a list of URIs to their
unresolved paths. For example, URIs passed to this RPC are mapped in the
following ways:

name: lookupResolvedPackageUris
desc:
The lookupResolvedPackageUris RPC is used to convert a list of URIs to
their resolved (or absolute) paths. For example, URIs passed to this RPC
are mapped in the following ways:

name: pause
desc:
The pause RPC is used to interrupt a running isolate. The RPC enqueues
the interrupt request and potentially returns before the isolate is
paused.

name: registerService
desc:
Registers a service that can be invoked by other VM service clients, where
service is the name of the service to advertise and alias is an
alternative name for the registered service.

name: registerServiceCallback
desc: Register a service for invocation.

name: reloadSources
desc:
The reloadSources RPC is used to perform a hot reload of the sources of
all isolates in the same isolate group as the isolate specified by
isolateId.

name: removeBreakpoint
desc: The removeBreakpoint RPC is used to remove a breakpoint by its id.

name: requestHeapSnapshot
desc: Requests a dump of the Dart heap of the given isolate.

name: resume
desc: The resume RPC is used to resume execution of a paused isolate.

name: setBreakpointState
desc:
The setBreakpointState RPC allows for breakpoints to be enabled or
disabled, without requiring for the breakpoint to be completely removed.

name: setExceptionPauseMode
desc:
The setExceptionPauseMode RPC is used to control if an isolate pauses
when an exception is thrown.

name: setFlag
desc:
The setFlag RPC is used to set a VM flag at runtime. Returns an error if
the named flag does not exist, the flag may not be set at runtime, or the
value is of the wrong type for the flag.

name: setIsolatePauseMode
desc:
The setIsolatePauseMode RPC is used to control if or when an isolate
will pause due to a change in execution state.

name: setLibraryDebuggable
desc:
The setLibraryDebuggable RPC is used to enable or disable whether
breakpoints and stepping work for a given library.

name: setName
desc: The setName RPC is used to change the debugging name for an isolate.

name: setTraceClassAllocation
desc:
The setTraceClassAllocation RPC allows for enabling or disabling
allocation tracing for a specific type of object. Allocation traces can be
retrieved with the getAllocationTraces RPC.

name: setVMName
desc: The setVMName RPC is used to change the debugging name for the vm.

name: setVMTimelineFlags
desc:
The setVMTimelineFlags RPC is used to set which timeline streams are
enabled.

name: streamCancel
desc: The streamCancel RPC cancels a stream subscription in the VM.

name: streamCpuSamplesWithUserTag
desc:
The streamCpuSamplesWithUserTag RPC is deprecated and calling it will
cause no effect. The RPC will return a Success object when called with
any string[] as the userTags argument, and will return an RPCError
when called with any other userTags argument.

name: streamListen
desc:
The streamListen RPC subscribes to a stream in the VM. Once subscribed,
the client will begin receiving events from the stream.

name: wrapFuture
desc: When overridden, this method wraps future with logic.

name: wsUri
desc: The web socket URI pointing to the target VM service instance.

name: decodeBase64
desc:
Decode a string in Base64 encoding into the equivalent non-encoded string.
This is useful for handling the results of the Stdout or Stderr events.

name: optional
desc: @optional

name: vmServiceConnectUri
desc: Connect to the given uri and return a new VmService instance.

name: vmServiceConnectUriWithFactory
desc:
Connect to the given uri and return a new instance of T, which is
constructed by vmServiceFactory and may be a subclass of VmService.
